# Questions in Livestream 188

Live Stream #188: Turning to Spilling the Coffee

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=903" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:03</a> | Did Egyptian princes practice stick-fighting arts such as tahtib? | He said generally no. Those arts show up more with slaves or lower-status fighters, while princes were trained in chariotry, archery, and elite military skills. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=1054" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:34</a> | How did the Mesopotamian transition from Akkadian to Aramaic affect what early archaeologists could know before cuneiform was deciphered? | He said Aramaic was already known, but the transition actually narrowed what could be learned because later Aramaic material did not preserve the older Akkadian cultural record. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=1723" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:43</a> | Is the Merneptah Stele really a problem for the late date of the Exodus? | He argued no, because the stele's portrayal of Israel still fits a late-exodus model and does not require an earlier conquest date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=3731" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:11</a> | Why did God give the Israelites the promised land? | He framed it as part of the promise to Abraham: posterity, land, and relationship with God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=3811" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:31</a> | How can Cain be a farmer if agriculture only developed a few thousand years ago and humans were hunter-gatherers before that? | He treated Cain and Abel as part of Genesis's farmer-versus-pastoralist pattern rather than a modern archaeological chronology claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=4633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:13</a> | What are your thoughts on the recent allegations against Michael Brown? | He said the allegations were very recent and urged waiting for the truth to come out rather than taking a side immediately. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=4930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:10</a> | Why is there a lack of Late Bronze II pottery at Jericho? | He said there is not a lack; Late Bronze IIB pottery is found across the site, which creates problems for some early-destruction arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=5408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:08</a> | What does fundamentalist Christmas curriculum usually get wrong? | He said the common error is placing the Magi at the manger; in the text they visit a house when Jesus is already a young child. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=6138" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:18</a> | Are you going to discuss Lorenzo Nigro's paper on the Late Bronze II destruction evidence? | He said yes, eventually, because the 2023 paper states much more explicitly what the earlier work implied. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=6511" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:31</a> | What commentary on Revelation would you recommend? | He recommended Grant Osborne's commentary as best for the overall dynamics of Revelation, even if not always for every detail. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=6859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:19</a> | Was Akhenaten's Aten religion monotheistic, polytheistic, monolatrous, or henotheistic? | He called it monolatrous pantheism: everything was treated as a manifestation of the Aten, but only the Aten was worshiped. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=6923" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:23</a> | Are there books that map faunal remains to Israelite borders and periods such as the Late Bronze and Iron Ages? | He said the work exists mostly in peer-reviewed papers rather than books, but he could not recall the specific papers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=7004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:44</a> | Was Potiphar a eunuch because he was captain of the guard in Genesis 39:1? | He said the biblical text does not say that, and Egyptian officials were not automatically made eunuchs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=7477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:37</a> | Why do your Terah and Abraham synchronisms give slightly different Hammurabi dates than older material? | He said the synchronisms have been refined over time, with only modest adjustments as new data is folded into the chronology work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=8921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:41</a> | Are the patriarchal names from Abraham to the twelve actual names or archetypal names? | He said we do not know for sure; they could be actual names, archetypal names, or wordplay, but the evidence is insufficient. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=9478" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:58</a> | How should inspiration be explained to people who think of it as a channeled document? | He said inspiration works through God's providential shaping of a person's life, experiences, education, and circumstances rather than simple dictation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=9859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:19</a> | Does the Reed Sea crossing require an even bigger secondary miracle, like giant walls of water at the Gulf of Aqaba? | He said no, calling that an overblown reading that misunderstands the text's wind-driven miracle and adds unnecessary extra miracles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=10491" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:51</a> | How were the Israelites affected, good and bad, by the Bronze Age Collapse? | He said they were overall winners because collapsing imperial powers gave Israel room to establish itself in Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=10609" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:49</a> | If Joseph worshiped God in Genesis, would he also have worshiped the gods of the Hyksos? | He said we do not know; Joseph may have served within a Hyksos religious environment, but the data are too limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=10690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:10</a> | If Israelites could not have anything with yeast during Passover, what about wine? | He said wine becomes an exception in later practice, with kosher wine heated to kill yeast, though earlier Israelite practice is harder to define. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=11184" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:24</a> | How does "Everlasting Father" in Isaiah 9:6 better translate as "Father of Eternity"? | He pointed to the Hebrew construct relationship, explaining that the phrase is better read as "father of eternity." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=11343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:03</a> | Where is Pi-hahiroth? | He said we do not know exactly, though Egyptian documents mention it and it was probably south of the Pelusiac estuary among a cluster of towns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=11563" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:43</a> | When does precaution become legalism? | He said precaution becomes legalism when it is made mandatory as a rule rather than treated as good advice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=12983" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:23</a> | What are good sources for BC-era maps, routes, topography, and related data? | He recommended Barry Beitzel's Moody Bible Atlas, while noting that other Bible atlases exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=13064" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:44</a> | Is 1 Peter 3 connected to the Nephilim? | He said the question needed a more exact reference, but tentatively discussed 1 Peter 3:18-20 and treated the connection as not straightforward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=13521" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:21</a> | Did Mary ride a donkey? | He said the Gospels do not say she did; it is probably an assumption from later retellings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=14135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:35</a> | Was David against Goliath more a shepherd tough-guy story than a Sunday-school little-guy story? | He said yes: David was a tough shepherd and skilled slinger, though still vulnerable if Goliath could close the distance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=14269" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:49</a> | Was Goliath's `Am I a dog, that you come to me with sticks?` just mockery, or was something more going on? | He treated it as a taunt meant to stir courage before combat and to provoke movement from otherwise hesitant armies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=14662" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:22</a> | Did Pontius Pilate die by old age or commit suicide? | He said we do not know; church tradition says suicide, but the records are not sufficient to confirm it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/aLsOi-ig6X4?t=14738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:38</a> | Was it fair for David to use a ranged weapon against Goliath? | He said yes; in one-on-one combat David could use a sling, and doing so carried real risk if he missed. |
